history.text: The Papal States began as a territorial power in central Italy.
The Kingdom of Italy captured Rome, ending Papal States rule.
The Lateran Treaty created Vatican City as a sovereign state.
A revised concordat updated relations between Italy and the Holy See.

worthSeeing.title: St. Peter's Basilica:
worthSeeing.text: The world's largest church, designed by Michelangelo and Bernini.

worthSeeing.title: Sistine Chapel:
worthSeeing.text: Michelangelo's ceiling fresco remains the most visited artwork on Earth.

worthSeeing.title: Vatican Museums:
worthSeeing.text: One of the world's richest museum collections, housed in papal palaces.

planningQuestions.q: What is Vatican City known for?
planningQuestions.a: St. Peter's Basilica, Sistine Chapel, Vatican Museums.
